mcqs.question: What is the index of the first element in a one-dimensional array?
mcqs.explanation: Array indexing in C and most programming languages starts at 0. The first element is accessed with index 0.
When an array is partially initialized, the remaining elements are automatically initialized to zero.

mcqs.question: Which of the following correctly declares an array of integers with 10 elements and initializes all to zero?
mcqs.explanation: Option B uses a shorthand where providing one zero initializer sets all elements to zero. Option C explicitly sets each element to zero. Both are syntactically correct and achieve the same result.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the linear search algorithm on an array of size n in the worst-case scenario?
mcqs.explanation: In the worst case, the target element is the last element or not present. The algorithm must check every single element once, leading to a time complexity of O(n).

mcqs.question: In C, if `int arr[5];` is declared and the address of `arr[0]` is 1000, what is the address of `arr[3]`? (Assume sizeof(int) = 4 bytes)
